expectations.text: References ISM Chapter 13 (System Hardening) as the relevant guideline
expectations.evidence: The response title is "ISM Chapter 13 — System Hardening: Requirements and Evidence for Windows Server 2022" and the overview section states "Chapter 13 sits within the Protect function of the ISM framework."

expectations.text: Covers OS hardening requirements: disable unnecessary services, remove/rename default accounts, apply ASD-approved hardening guide
expectations.evidence: Section 1 (Operating System Hardening) covers: "Remove all Windows roles, role services, and features that are not required", "Disable all default and built-in accounts that are not needed. The built-in local Administrator account must be renamed", and "Apply the ASD hardening guidance for Windows Server. ASD publishes a dedicated hardening guide (Hardening Microsoft Windows Server)."

expectations.text: Mentions application allow-listing as a key ISM/Essential Eight control
expectations.evidence: "This is one of the highest-priority Chapter 13 controls and maps directly to the Essential Eight 'Application control' strategy." Section 3 is dedicated to Application Control (Allow-Listing).

expectations.text: States least privilege principle: no standing administrative access; use of Privileged Access Management (PAM)
expectations.evidence: "No standing administrative access. Standard user accounts must be used for day-to-day operations; administrative credentials must only be used when performing administrative tasks." The section is titled "Least Privilege and Privileged Access Management" and covers PAM concepts including JIT access, PAWs, and LAPS.

expectations.text: Lists evidence types required: hardening checklists, configuration screenshots or CIS benchmark scan results, PAM solution logs, software allow-list policy
expectations.evidence: Evidence section covers: hardening checklists ("Application hardening checklists", "ASD Windows Server Hardening Guide gap comparison"), configuration screenshots ("Group Policy export or baseline configuration report", "BIOS/UEFI configuration screenshots"), PAM-related evidence ("Active Directory group membership reports", "LAPS deployment confirmation", "Privileged account review records"), and allow-list policy ("Application control strategy document" describes "what is allowed, how exceptions are handled"). CIS benchmark scan results are not explicitly named — ASD hardening guide gap comparison is the equivalent referenced.
